slots: Deccan plateau approach, crater first glimpse
Leave Chhatrapati Sambhajinagar early for the three-hour drive to Buldhana district. Stop at a highway dhaba for breakfast — the crater rim appears suddenly above the plain.
Impact geology, temple ruins, saline lake views
Walk the rim trail with a local guide who can explain the basalt impact uniqueness. Visit shore temples and photograph the lake’s shifting green hues.
Explore the Hemadpanti Daitya Sudan temple in town, then return to the rim after dark for stars — minimal light pollution.
Sunrise transforms the crater — mist often fills the bowl before burning off. Bring binoculars for waders on the alkaline shore.
Visit any open interpretation displays and the Kamalja Devi temple at the water’s edge — respectful dress required.
Drive back with optional Himroo or Paithani shopping in the city.
Many travellers combine Lonar with the timeless icons of Ajanta or Ellora — plan an extra day if time allows.
If routing through Aurangabad, add Daulatabad’s Andheri tunnel climb for a second wonder in one trip.
Lonar rewards unhurried pacing — one full day minimum on the crater itself.
